text: Base pricing includes a clearly enumerated set of features (core functionality, defined usage quotas, standard support). Add-ons are explicitly listed with unit pricing (e.g., per user/API call/GB) and no hidden fees.
Overages are defined with precise formulas and thresholds (e.g., $X per 1k requests beyond quota). A total cost calculator and sample invoices are provided for common scenarios.
Pricing increases are contractually capped (e.g., ≤X% annually) with Y months advance notice. Mid-term price changes are not allowed; adjustments apply only at renewal with agreed limits.
We disclose total cost of ownership, including implementation, migration, support tiers, data egress, and premium features. We provide a detailed example bill for a representative workload.
We provide general pricing tiers, but detailed inclusions, add-ons, and unit pricing are not fully itemized.
Pricing depends on usage, but the exact metrics, thresholds, and formulas for overages are not clearly defined.
Additional fees may apply (e.g., support, storage), but a complete list and costs are not transparently documented.
Price changes are possible at renewal, but caps, notice periods, and mid-contract protections are not clearly specified.
Pricing is not disclosed; customers must contact sales for details.
Pricing depends on usage without defining units, thresholds, or formulas.
Additional fees may apply, but they are not listed or quantified.
Prices are subject to change without caps or notice periods.
